questions: Our client is a coffee chain whose profits have fallen 20% this year. How would you structure your approach to finding the cause?
Estimate the number of cars sold in India in a year. Walk me through your reasoning out loud.
A retail client is deciding whether to enter the online grocery market. What factors would you analyse?
How would you improve the profitability of a struggling movie theatre?
Tell me about a time you solved a complex problem with limited information. How did you structure it?
Walk me through how you would size the market for electric two-wheelers in India.
A client wants to cut costs by 15%. How would you identify where to cut without hurting the business?
How do you communicate a recommendation when the data is ambiguous?

rubric.strong: Top-down structured frameworks (MECE), states hypotheses, does clean back-of-envelope math out loud, commercially sensible, summarises recommendation clearly, checks assumptions.
rubric.weak: Unstructured rambling, no framework, math errors or avoids numbers, no clear recommendation, ignores business context.
